Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] ### Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] ### Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == ### National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze {{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == ### National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | Alternate name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | Latitude = 36.745258 | Longitude = 140.135847 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} | Size = Mound length 60.5 m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} direct burial | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, iron artifacts, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-West | Label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] ### Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] ### Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts

It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == ### National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | Alternate name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | Latitude = 36.745258 | Longitude = 140.135847 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} | Size = Mound length 60.5 m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} direct burial | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, iron artifacts, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-West | Label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] ### Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] ### Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == ### National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa (Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | Latitude = 36.745258 | Longitude = 140.135847 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} | Size = Mound length 60.5 m | Burial Facility = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} direct burial | Artifacts = Bronze mirrors, iron artifacts, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-West | Label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] ### Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] ### Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == ### National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] ### Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] ### Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == ### National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] ### Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] ### Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun#Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === === Characteristics === - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === === National Historic Site === - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. - '''Construction Features:''' - Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] - Terrace structure carved out around the mound. - Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] - Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] - Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] - Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] - '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. - '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] - '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] - '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] - '''Rear section:''' - Length: 31.5 meters. - '''Front section:''' - Length: 29 meters. - Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] - '''Bronze Mirror:''' - Yatakahoko mirror (1). - '''Iron Artifacts:''' - Sword (1). - Saw (1). - Chisels (4). - Small knives (2). - Axe (1). - Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. - '''Shape:''' Square. - '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] - '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west.* '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History ==* '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10]* '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.* '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11]* '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13]* '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14]* '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters.* '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15]* '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1).* '''Iron Artifacts:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970.* '''Shape:''' Square.* '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16]* '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site ===* '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities ==* {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun.* Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west.* '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History ==* '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10]* '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.* '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11]* '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13]* '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14]* '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters.* '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15]* '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1).* '''Iron Artifacts:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970.* '''Shape:''' Square.* '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16]* '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun.* Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west.* '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History ==* '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10]* '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site.* '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11]* '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13]* '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14]* '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters.* '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron Artifacts:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''Shape:''' Square. * '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun.* Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron Artifacts:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''Shape:''' Square. * '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** Burial facility: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** Dimensions of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** Artifacts: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron artifacts (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === Construction Period === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial artifacts (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == Artifacts == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron Artifacts:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Tumulus) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''Shape:''' Square. * '''Dimensions:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was designated a Tochigi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953 and a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron artifacts from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References ==
