{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = National {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural other_designation. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National other_designation (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefectural other_designation in 1953 and a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * {{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefectural other_designation in 1953 and a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural other_designation. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National other_designation (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware

It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' Designation as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' Designation as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun (Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}

It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware

{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}

It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"

Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}
