revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 那須八幡塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に後方部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = 八幡塚1号墳 |所属 = [[那須小川古墳群]]
（吉田新宿古墳群/八幡塚古墳群） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 44|緯度秒 = 42.93 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 8|経度秒 = 9.05 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長60.5m |埋葬施設 = [[割竹形木棺]]直葬 |出土品 = 銅鏡・鉄製品・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「那須八幡塚古墳群」
（「[[那須小川古墳群]]」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル = 那須八幡塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''那須八幡塚古墳'''（なすはちまんづかこふん、八幡塚1号墳）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]（うち吉田新宿古墳群・八幡塚古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 本項では、那須八幡塚古墳の南にある'''[[#吉田富士山古墳|吉田富士山古墳]]'''（八幡塚2号墳）についても解説する。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |[[上侍塚古墳]]||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |[[上侍塚北古墳]]||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||[[駒形大塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||'''那須八幡塚古墳'''||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[前方後方墳]]2基・[[方墳]]20基以上からなる吉田新宿古墳群の南部に位置する{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）・[[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周囲には削り出しによるテラスを有し、その外側には不整形の周溝が巡らされる。周溝からは多数の[[土師器]]が検出されている{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における[[割竹形木棺]]の直葬で、木棺主軸を墳丘主軸と平行方向として、長さ4.75メートル・幅0.3メートルを測り、東西の小口には粘土塊が置かれる{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として銅鏡（八鳳鏡）・鉄製品（剣・鋸・鉇・小刀・斧・鎌）などが検出されている{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代前期の後半代と推定される{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。那須地方では近代以降に最初に発掘調査された古墳であるとともに、舶載鏡を副葬する前方後方墳として注目された古墳である{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 古墳域は[[2002年]]（平成14年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、埋葬施設の発掘調査。副葬品出土（小川町古代文化研究会、[[1957年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）5月6日、栃木県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）、墳形確認の発掘調査（小川町教育委員会、[[1997年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘（左に後方部、右に前方部）}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：60.5メートル（または62メートル史跡説明板。） * 後方部 ** 長さ：31.5メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：29メートル ** 幅：16メートル File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む == 出土品 == [[ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|八鳳鏡}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 1953年（昭和28年）の調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * 銅鏡 1 ** 八鳳鏡 1 * 鉄製品 ** 剣 1 ** 鋸 1 ** 鉇 4 ** 小刀 2 ** 斧 1 ** 鎌 2 ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|鉄製品
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|八鳳鏡（複製）・土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|鉄製品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 吉田富士山古墳 == '''吉田富士山古墳'''（よしだふじやまこふん、八幡塚2号墳）は、那須八幡塚古墳の南にある古墳。形状は[[方墳]]。吉田新宿古墳群の南端に位置する。現在は墳頂に富士浅間神社が鎮座する。[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に周溝の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で、東西約25メートル・南北約20メートル。高さ約3メートルを測る{{Sfn|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。墳丘周囲では北・西・南側で周溝が認められる{{Sfn|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。築造時期は明らかでないが、一帯の古墳群と同様の前期古墳と推定される{{Sfn|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（昭和28年）11月10日に栃木県指定史跡に指定され、2002年（平成14年）に国の史跡に指定された（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。現在は工場敷地内で保存されている。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 那須八幡塚古墳群（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[東京国立博物館]]（[[東京都]][[台東区]]） - 那須八幡塚古墳の出土鏡・鉄製品を保管。 * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 那須八幡塚古墳の出土土器を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（那珂川町教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=「那須八幡塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「富士山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=八幡塚古墳 > 那須八幡塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|那須八幡塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=2002|chapter=八幡塚古墳 > 那須八幡塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1957|chapter=|title= 那須八幡塚古墳|publisher=小川町古代文化研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1971|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/88552 |title=吉田富士山古墳|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=|title= 那須八幡塚古墳（小川町埋蔵文化財調査報告 第10集）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[那須小川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}} * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html 那須小川古墳群]、[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/chizu/kuni/hachimanduka.html 那須八幡塚古墳群] - 那珂川町ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=吉田富士山古墳 |1-1=栃木県の古墳 |1-2=那珂川町の歴史 |1-3=方墳 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:なすはちまんつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[那須八幡塚古墳]] to [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 那須八幡塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に後方部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = 八幡塚1号墳 |所属 = [[那須小川古墳群]]
（吉田新宿古墳群/八幡塚古墳群） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 44|緯度秒 = 42.93 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 8|経度秒 = 9.05 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長60.5m |埋葬施設 = [[割竹形木棺]]直葬 |出土品 = 銅鏡・鉄製品・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「那須八幡塚古墳群」
（「[[那須小川古墳群]]」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル = 那須八幡塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''那須八幡塚古墳'''（なすはちまんづかこふん、八幡塚1号墳）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]（うち吉田新宿古墳群・八幡塚古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 本項では、那須八幡塚古墳の南にある'''[[#吉田富士山古墳|吉田富士山古墳]]'''（八幡塚2号墳）についても解説する。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |[[上侍塚古墳]]||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |[[上侍塚北古墳]]||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||[[駒形大塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||'''那須八幡塚古墳'''||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[前方後方墳]]2基・[[方墳]]20基以上からなる吉田新宿古墳群の南部に位置する{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）・[[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周囲には削り出しによるテラスを有し、その外側には不整形の周溝が巡らされる。周溝からは多数の[[土師器]]が検出されている{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における[[割竹形木棺]]の直葬で、木棺主軸を墳丘主軸と平行方向として、長さ4.75メートル・幅0.3メートルを測り、東西の小口には粘土塊が置かれる{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として銅鏡（八鳳鏡）・鉄製品（剣・鋸・鉇・小刀・斧・鎌）などが検出されている{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 築造時期は、古墳時代前期の後半代と推定される{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。那須地方では近代以降に最初に発掘調査された古墳であるとともに、舶載鏡を副葬する前方後方墳として注目された古墳である{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 古墳域は[[2002年]]（平成14年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）、埋葬施設の発掘調査。副葬品出土（小川町古代文化研究会、[[1957年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）5月6日、栃木県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）、墳形確認の発掘調査（小川町教育委員会、[[1997年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘（左に後方部、右に前方部）}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：60.5メートル（または62メートル史跡説明板。） * 後方部 ** 長さ：31.5メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：29メートル ** 幅：16メートル File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む == 出土品 == [[ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|八鳳鏡}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 1953年（昭和28年）の調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * 銅鏡 1 ** 八鳳鏡 1 * 鉄製品 ** 剣 1 ** 鋸 1 ** 鉇 4 ** 小刀 2 ** 斧 1 ** 鎌 2 ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|鉄製品
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|八鳳鏡（複製）・土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:那須八幡塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|鉄製品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 吉田富士山古墳 == '''吉田富士山古墳'''（よしだふじやまこふん、八幡塚2号墳）は、那須八幡塚古墳の南にある古墳。形状は[[方墳]]。吉田新宿古墳群の南端に位置する。現在は墳頂に富士浅間神社が鎮座する。[[1970年]]（[[昭和]]45年）に周溝の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で、東西約25メートル・南北約20メートル。高さ約3メートルを測る{{Sfn|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。墳丘周囲では北・西・南側で周溝が認められる{{Sfn|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。築造時期は明らかでないが、一帯の古墳群と同様の前期古墳と推定される{{Sfn|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（昭和28年）11月10日に栃木県指定史跡に指定され、2002年（平成14年）に国の史跡に指定された（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。現在は工場敷地内で保存されている。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 那須八幡塚古墳群（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[東京国立博物館]]（[[東京都]][[台東区]]） - 那須八幡塚古墳の出土鏡・鉄製品を保管。 * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 那須八幡塚古墳の出土土器を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（那珂川町教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=「那須八幡塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|那須八幡塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「富士山古墳」|ref={{Harvid|富士山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=八幡塚古墳 > 那須八幡塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|那須八幡塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=2002|chapter=八幡塚古墳 > 那須八幡塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|那須八幡塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - (Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}}

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}} Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{citation needed|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]]

It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]]

It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]]

{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]]
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]] Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]]
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]] Yoshida | coordinates = 36.745258 | coordinates = 140.135847 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]]

It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods

{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]]

Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]]

It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}

It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] }} [[sn:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:asu Hachimanzuka Kofun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}
