{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]]
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]] Yoshida | coordinates = {{Coord|36.745361|N|140.135972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]]
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]] Yoshida | coordinates = {{Coord|36.745361|N|140.135972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]] Yoshida | coordinates = {{Coord|36.745361|N|140.135972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"

Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} Yoshida | coordinates = {{Coord|36.745361|N|140.135972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun]][[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (rear section on the right, front section in the back left) | alternate_name = Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Hachimanzuka Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} Yoshida | coordinates = {{Coord|36.745361|N|140.135972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length 60.5 m | burial = {{ill|Waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} direct burial | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, iron burial goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-West | map_dot_label = Nasu Hachimanzuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' (那須八幡塚古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby [[Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun=Yoshida Fujisan Kofun|Yoshida Fujisan Kofun]] (Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right)}}]] The Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun is located on a terrace on the right bank of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the southern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli.[1] Excavations were conducted in 1953 and 1991.[2] === Characteristics === * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented west. * '''built Features:''' ** Fukiishi (stone covering) detected.[3] ** Terrace structure carved out around the mound. ** Irregularly shaped moat surrounding the mound.[4] ** burial: Direct burial in a {{ill|waritakegata wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} at the rear section's summit.[5] ** area of the wooden coffin: 4.75 meters in length and 0.3 meters in width, aligned parallel to the mound axis.[6] ** burial goods: Bronze mirrors (e.g., Yatakahoko mirror), iron burial goods (e.g., swords, saws, chisels, small knives, axes, sickles), and haji ware pottery.[7] === built === The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the latter half of the early Kofun period.[8] It was the first tumulus in the Nasu region to be excavated in modern times and is notable for the inclusion of imported bronze mirrors.[9] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == History == * '''1953:''' Excavation of the burial facility; discovery of burial burial goods (reported by the Ogawa Town Ancient Culture Research Association in 1957).[10] * '''1961 (May 6):''' designation1 as a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site. * '''1991:''' Additional structural excavations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education (reported in 1997).[11] * '''2002 (December 19):''' designation1 as a National Historic site (as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster).[12] == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:[13] * '''Total length:''' 60.5 meters (or 62 meters according to some sources).[14] * '''Rear section:''' ** Length: 31.5 meters. * '''Front section:''' ** Length: 29 meters. ** Width: 16 meters. File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear section File:Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front section == burial goods == [[File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 八鳳鏡 (J-36999-1).JPG|thumb|200x200px|{{center|Yatakahoko mirror}}{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.}}]] burial goods discovered during the 1953 excavation include:[15] * '''Bronze Mirror:''' ** Yatakahoko mirror (1). * '''Iron burial goods:''' ** Sword (1). ** Saw (1). ** Chisels (4). ** Small knives (2). ** Axe (1). ** Sickles (2). File:那須八幡塚古墳出土 鉄製品 (J-36999-2,3,4,6,7,8,9).JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Tokyo National Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|Yatakahoko mirror (replica) and haji ware
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} File:那須八幡塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Iron burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at the Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Yoshida Fujisan Kofun == '''Yoshida Fujisan Kofun''' (吉田富士山古墳, Hachimanzuka No. 2 Kofun) lies to the south of Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. It is a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located at the southernmost end of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group. The summit now hosts a Fuji Sengen Shrine. Excavations were conducted in 1970. * '''type:''' Square. * '''area:''' Approximately 25 meters east-west, 20 meters north-south; 3 meters in height.[16] * '''Features:''' Moat surrounding the north, west, and south sides.[17] The tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period, like others in the group. It was Historic site a Tochigi Prefectural Historic site in 1953 and a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group''' (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster): Designated on December 19, 2002.[18] == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}} (Tokyo, Taito): Houses bronze mirrors and iron burial goods from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. * Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits pottery from the Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster] - Nakagawa Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|那須八幡塚古墳|version=99994935|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:那須八幡塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q18337611}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]
